_doc: Proof-of-Prompt validation. Each role prompt is validated against the JSON Schema below, sent to the LLM via response_format={'type':'json_schema','schema':<this>}. The class is the schema, validator, and persistence format.

perception.notes: No goal-id field (positional identity); artifact_index is an integer, never a raw art: handle.

PerceivedGoal.description:
A goal as the Perception model is allowed to express it.

NOTE the absence of any id field: goals are identified positionally.
`artifact_index` is an INTEGER index into the indexed memory-hit artifacts
presented in the prompt — never a raw `art:...` string. The loop maps the
integer back to a real handle (and drops it if it doesn't exist).
PerceivedGoal.title: PerceivedGoal

schema.description: Top-level structured output of a Perception call.
schema.title: PerceptionOutput

decision.auto_route: decision (router picks tier; bypassed to AGENT_PROVIDER for large attachments)
decision.notes: Decision uses the gateway's NATIVE function-calling: the model either emits a tool call (provider fills arguments as a real object) or plain text. decision.py validates the result into DecisionOutput (answer XOR tool_call). Native calling avoids the empty-{}/mangled-name failures that response_format JSON produces for an open arguments dict.

ToolCall.description:
Exactly one MCP tool invocation (INTERNAL boundary — arguments is a real
dict the loop hands to Action).
ToolCall.title: ToolCall

DecisionOutput.description:
Decision returns EITHER a final answer OR exactly one tool call —
never both, never two tools, never narration.

The model may, being a weak router-tier model, occasionally populate both
fields. The `mode="after"` validator normalizes to the invariant rather
than crashing the loop: a populated tool name wins; otherwise the answer
stands; an empty output raises (caller treats it as a failed step).
DecisionOutput.title: DecisionOutput

ToolCall.description:
Exactly one MCP tool invocation (INTERNAL boundary — arguments is a real
dict the loop hands to Action).
ToolCall.title: ToolCall

memory_classify.notes: The ONLY LLM call in Memory. Reads and record_outcome use no LLM.

schema.description: Schema for the single classify call in remember().
schema.title: _Classification
